_comment: operon-plugin hooks. Phase 2 wires SessionStart to bind_handle per SPEC.md section 8 and section 6.5 step 6. The mcp_tool handler form invokes mcp__operon__bind_handle directly. Phase 2 fix: schema corrections per https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks -- (1) `server` field is REQUIRED for mcp_tool hooks (was missing, Claude Code's plugin validator rejects the file); (2) the arguments-object field is `input`, NOT `arguments`; (3) `${env:VAR}` interpolation is NOT supported in mcp_tool input, so the handle parameter is dropped from the hook -- bind_handle reads OPERON_AGENT_HANDLE from its own subprocess env via identity.read_env_handle() and validates against the hook-supplied session_id. The matcher 'startup|resume|clear' fires on all SessionStart sources; bind_handle is idempotent so resume/clear re-fires against the same handle succeed.
